title: Optimization Workflow, Generalization, and Decision Policy
description:
## Summary
Operationalize score optimization as a staged workflow that combines fixed deterministic evidence, random generalization evidence, malfunction analysis, and explicit decision policy.

## Scope
- Candidate score changes are assessed with both fixed deterministic datasets and random-sample evaluations.
- The workflow produces an assessment bundle rather than relying on one raw evaluation in isolation.
- The workflow computes comparison signals such as generalization gap and stability/variance across random runs.
- The workflow decides whether to keep optimizing, stop for SME clarification, route upstream remediation, or fix a mechanical/system issue.

## Key assumptions
- This epic depends on deterministic dataset infrastructure, reliable evaluation/RCA execution, and misclassification classification.
- Public-facing web app documentation is required as part of this workflow.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
## Story to task mapping

This epic's stories are implemented by the following task groups:

- `plx-aebf38` Assess candidate score changes with fixed and random evaluation evidence
  - `plx-e52e77` staged optimization protocol
  - `plx-076ad2` candidate assessment bundle definition
  - `plx-22533f` staged runner / bundle recording workflow

- `plx-eab317` Route optimization work based on malfunction mix and evaluation flags
  - `plx-3e14e5` workflow decision integration
  - `plx-eb6f72` dashboard surfaces for workflow decisions and bundle evidence

- `plx-fe090f` Track benchmark-versus-random generalization gap and stability
  - `plx-cface0` generalization-gap and stability metrics
  - `plx-eb6f72` dashboard KPI surfaces

## Foundation reminders
- Fixed deterministic dataset roles come from `plx-25e99b` in the deterministic-datasets epic.
- Reliable evaluation execution comes from `plx-9aa370` and related RCA-contract work in the reliability epic.
- Malfunction analysis inputs come from the misclassification-triage epic, especially `plx-c7fda3`.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Locked workflow decisions for operationalization: (1) fast-loop plus hard-gate cadence: random n=50 for iterative loops and required random n=200 confirmation gate before accept decisions; default window 180 days. (2) Candidate assessment bundle persistence: full canonical JSON in task/evaluation attachment plus compact summary fields for dashboard/query speed. (3) Keep acceptance defaults unchanged: min_reference_delta=0.01 and max_generalization_drop=0.02. (4) No champion promotion in this workflow.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Epic completed: all child stories/tasks for workflow operationalization are now closed (staged 50/200 protocol, bundle contract + persistence, decision routing, KPI metrics/surfaces, docs, HITL dashboard path).
